title: Relationships & Migrations
description: Define model relationships with @Relationship, handle data migrations with VersionedSchema and SchemaMigrationPlan, and manage deletes with cascade rules.

sections.content:
## Model Relationships

SwiftData models can reference other models using the `@Relationship` property wrapper. Unlike Core Data's relationships defined in a graph editor, SwiftData relationships are declared inline in your Swift code.

### One-to-Many

```swift
@Model
class Author {
    var name: String
    @Relationship(deleteRule: .cascade)
    var books: [Book]
}

@Model
class Book {
    var title: String
    var author: Author?
}
```

The `deleteRule: .cascade` means when an `Author` is deleted, all their books are deleted too.

### Delete Rules

| Rule | Behavior |
|------|----------|
| `.cascade` | Delete the related objects too |
| `.deny` | Prevent deletion if related objects exist |
| `.nullify` | Set the inverse relationship to nil |
| `.noAction` | Leave related objects unchanged (can cause orphans) |

### Inverse Relationships

SwiftData automatically infers inverse relationships when there are two properties pointing at each other. The `@Relationship(inverse:\\Book.author)` annotation is optional — use it when inference fails or you have multiple relationships between the same types.

sections.title: Schema Versioning
sections.expectedOutput:
v1 < v2: true
v2 < v3: true
Migration path: v1 -> v2 -> v3

sections.question: What does the .cascade delete rule do in a SwiftData @Relationship?
options: Prevents deletion of the parent object
Sets the inverse relationship to nil
Deletes the related objects when the parent is deleted
sections.explanation: The .cascade delete rule automatically deletes all related objects when the parent is deleted, keeping data consistent.

sections.question: Which SwiftData type defines the steps to migrate data between schema versions?
sections.explanation: SchemaMigrationPlan defines the ordered schemas and migration stages that describe how to transform data from one version to another.
